word_list.word: a leopard cannot change its spots
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ lˈɛpɚd kænˈɑːt tʃˈeɪndʒ ɪts spˈɑːts

senses.definition: used to imply that a person's fundamental nature or character is difficult and unlikely to change, even if they try

word_list.word: an ape is an ape, a varlet is a varlet, though they be clad in silk or scarlet
word_list.pronunciation: ɐn ˈeɪp ɪz ɐn ˈeɪp ɐ vˈɑːɹlət ɪz ɐ vˈɑːɹlət ðˌoʊ ðeɪ biː klˈæd ɪn sˈɪlk ɔːɹ skˈɑːɹlət

senses.definition: used to imply that a person's true nature or identity cannot be disguised or changed by outward appearances, such as clothing or social status

word_list.word: the wolf (may|might) lose his teeth, but never his nature
word_list.pronunciation: ðə wˈʊlf mˈeɪ mˌaɪt lˈuːz hɪz tˈiːθ bˌʌt nˈɛvɚ hɪz nˈeɪtʃɚ

senses.definition: used to emphasize that a person or creature's fundamental qualities and character are innate and unlikely to change, despite the passage of time or the influence of external factors

word_list.word: the fox (may|might) grow grey, but never good
word_list.pronunciation: ðə fˈɑːks mˈeɪ mˌaɪt ɡɹˈoʊ ɡɹˈeɪ bˌʌt nˈɛvɚ ɡˈʊd

senses.definition: used to suggest that a person's inherent character or nature is unlikely to change, even with age or experience
used to suggest that certain qualities or abilities are innate and cannot be acquired through training or education

word_list.word: you cannot make a silk purse out of a sow's ear
word_list.pronunciation: juː kænˈɑːt mˌeɪk ɐ sˈɪlk pˈɜːs ˌaʊɾəv ɐ sˈoʊz ˈɪɹ

senses.definition: used to suggest that one cannot turn something of low quality or little value into something of high quality or great value, no matter how much effort is put into it
used to suggest that a person's innate qualities and traits have a greater impact on their development and behavior than external factors such as upbringing or environment

examples.x: The coach believed that nature passes nurture, and that some athletes are just naturally more talented than others, regardless of their training and practice.

word_list.word: a crow is never whiter for washing itself often
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ kɹˈoʊ ɪz nˈɛvɚ wˈaɪɾɚ fɔːɹ wˈɑːʃɪŋ ɪtsˈɛlf ˈɔfən

senses.definition: used to imply that a person's inherent character or nature cannot be changed by changing their external appearance or behavior

examples.x: Don't be fooled by his attempts to appear trustworthy; a crow is never whiter for washing itself often.

word_list.word: send a fool to market and a fool he (will|shall) return
word_list.pronunciation: sˈɛnd ɐ fˈuːl tə mˈɑːɹkɪt ænd ɐ fˈuːl hiː wɪl ʃˌæl ɹɪtˈɜːn

senses.definition: used to imply that a foolish person will remain foolish no matter how many opportunities or experiences they have

word_list.word: a bellowing cow soon forgets her calf
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ bˈɛloʊɪŋ kˈaʊ sˈuːn fɚɡˈɛts hɜː kˈæf

senses.definition: used to highlight the tendency of individuals to quickly lose interest or forget about something or someone they once cared deeply about

examples.x: Maggie had been committed to a project, but when it became too challenging, she gave up and moved on, proving that a bellowing cow soon forgets her calf.
Maria had been part of a social group, but when she had a falling out with one of the members, they quickly excluded her and moved on, proving that a bellowing cow soon forgets her calf.

word_list.word: we are born crying, live complaining, and die disappointed
word_list.pronunciation: wiː ɑːɹ bˈɔːɹn kɹˈaɪɪŋ lˈaɪv kəmplˈeɪnɪŋ ænd dˈaɪ dˌɪsɐpˈɔɪntᵻd

senses.definition: used to suggest that from the moment of our birth, we express dissatisfaction and continue to do so throughout our lives, often ending with unfulfilled expectations and disappointment

word_list.word: what is bred in the bone (will|shall) come out in the flesh
word_list.pronunciation: wˌʌt ɪz bɹˈɛd ɪnðə bˈoʊn wɪl ɔːɹ ʃˌæl kˈʌm ˈaʊt ɪnðə flˈɛʃ

senses.definition: used to suggest that a person's innate tendencies or characteristics, inherited from their ancestors, will eventually become apparent in their actions or personality and are difficult to change

examples.x: Despite her best efforts to be kind and compassionate, her innate selfishness couldn't be suppressed, proving that what is bred in the bone will come out in the flesh.
